item.title: As we push the frontiers of AI infrastructure, our research explores a future where training isn’t c...
item.description:
As we push the frontiers of AI infrastructure, our research explores a future where training isn’t constrained by geography, capacity or type of chip.
Dive into the technical details → https://t.co/tAq2nQ6kTa https://t.co/y49hOiucXf
--- @GoogleDeepMind | ❤️ 15 | 🔁 0 | 💬 0 | 👁 2878
item.author: @GoogleDeepMind

item.title: This progress allow us to rethink global compute: 🔘 We successfully trained a 12B @GoogleGemma model...
item.description:
This progress allow us to rethink global compute: 🔘 We successfully trained a 12B @GoogleGemma model across four US regions using low-bandwidth networks 🔘 We showed we can mix different hardware generations, such as TPU6e and TPUv5p, without slowing down performance during training
--- @GoogleDeepMind | ❤️ 19 | 🔁 0 | 💬 1 | 👁 3045
item.author: @GoogleDeepMind

item.title: This is Decoupled DiLoCo: our new resilient and flexible way to train advanced AI models across mult...
item.author: @GoogleDeepMind

item.title:
Video editing is a huge market - creators are willing to pay hundreds to get a great clip.
That's w...
item.description:
Video editing is a huge market - creators are willing to pay hundreds to get a great clip.
That's why I'm excited about products like Cappy from @trymirage, the first ai video editor that you can simply text to create or edit a video.
It also adds engaging captions and voiceovers. Below's a clip I made of a corgi as an example.
Check out Cappy here: https://t.co/xUe4yKvpx9
--- @petergyang | ❤️ 4 | 🔁 0 | 💬 1 | 👁 1131
item.author: @petergyang

item.title:
The third lesson is the one every C-level team should pay attention to right now:
In the age of AI,...
item.description:
The third lesson is the one every C-level team should pay attention to right now:
In the age of AI, governance teams cannot act like the police.
Agents, dashboards, and data products are multiplying fast. If governance slows the business down, the business will route around it.
The real goal is to become a partner to innovation: → make trusted data easier to find → make safe usage clearer → make new data products faster to launch
That is the big takeaway from this video.
Watch the full conversation.
What do you think is the main reason most enterprise data governance efforts stall, strategy, ownership, or trying to scale before the foundation is ready?
--- @Ronald_vanLoon | ❤️ 0 | 🔁 0 | 💬 0 | 👁 79
item.author: @Ronald_vanLoon

item.title:
The second lesson:
Think big, start small, prove it end-to-end.
What worked here was not building ...
item.description:
The second lesson:
Think big, start small, prove it end-to-end.
What worked here was not building half a glossary and hoping people would adopt it.
They built the foundations properly: → business glossary → lineage → roles and ownership → an end-to-end setup that worked for one business first
Then they scaled.
That matters because adoption follows credibility. If the system is incomplete, the business ignores it. If it works in practice, usage grows naturally.
--- @Ronald_vanLoon | ❤️ 0 | 🔁 0 | 💬 1 | 👁 84
item.author: @Ronald_vanLoon

item.title:
Most data governance programs do not fail because of bad technology.
They fail because leaders trea...
item.description:
Most data governance programs do not fail because of bad technology.
They fail because leaders treat governance like a software rollout, when it is really an organizational change effort.
In this conversation I explored with Grimme Bogaerts & Martijn Vanhauwaert from Colruyt Group, one line stood out to me:
"It's not about the tooling."
That mindset is why they scaled to 5,500 users.
Here’s what most executives still get wrong, and what actually worked, with insights from @collibra.
#CollibraPartner
--- @Ronald_vanLoon | ❤️ 3 | 🔁 0 | 💬 2 | 👁 151
item.author: @Ronald_vanLoon

item.title:
“Every single thing that we learned on ISS is up here." - @Astro_Christina
For over 25 years,...
item.description:
“Every single thing that we learned on ISS is up here." - @Astro_Christina
For over 25 years, the station has provided a platform for sharpening the skills, tech, and knowledge needed to thrive on journeys to the Moon and beyond. More: https://t.co/0ZrcIpE2KR https://t.co/FvdNoxZwvK

item.title:
Cool paper on diversity collapse in AI agents.
It's a common issue with all the deployed multi-agen...
item.description:
Cool paper on diversity collapse in AI agents.
It's a common issue with all the deployed multi-agent systems.
New paper shows that multi-agent LLM systems converge on near-identical outputs over time, even across different architectures and different starting prompts. They call it diversity collapse. The cause is structural coupling. Shared context, shared task descriptions, and mutual feedback pull everyone toward the same attractor.
They measure it formally with metrics like the Vendi score, and the homogenization is real.
Which means the whole sales pitch for multi-agent on creative tasks (brainstorming, hypothesis generation, ideation) partially falls apart unless you explicitly engineer against it. That means having isolated reasoning phases, decoupled evaluation, and heterogeneous agent designs.
If you're running a multi-agent flow on creative work and you haven't tested for this, there's a real chance you're paying five models to produce one answer in a trench coat.
Paper: https://t.co/sSXb8SOdd8
Learn to build effective AI agents in our academy: https://t.co/LRnpZN7L4c
--- @dair_ai | ❤️ 12 | 🔁 4 | 💬 2 | 👁 2471
item.author: @dair_ai
